sentences.text: RISC-V defines a separate address space of 4096 Control and Status registers associated with each hart.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0

sentences.text: This chapter defines the full set of CSR instructions that operate on these CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0

sentences.text: All CSR instructions atomically read-modify-write a single CSR, whose CSR specifier is encoded in the 12-bit csr field of the instruction held in bits 31-20.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The immediate forms use a 5-bit zero-extended immediate encoded in the rs1 field.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The CSRRW (Atomic Read/Write CSR) instruction atomically swaps values in the CSRs and integer registers. CSRRW reads the old value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits, then writes it to integer register rd.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The initial value in rs1 is written to the CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: If rd=`x0`, then the instruction shall not read the CSR and shall not cause any of the side effects that might occur on a CSR read.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The CSRRS (Atomic Read and Set Bits in CSR) instruction reads the value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits, and writes it to integer register rd.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The initial value in integer register rs1 is treated as a bit mask that specifies bit positions to be set in the CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Any bit that is high in rs1 will cause the corresponding bit to be set in the CSR, if that CSR bit is writable.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The CSRRC (Atomic Read and Clear Bits in CSR) instruction reads the value of the CSR, zero-extends the value to XLEN bits, and writes it to integer register rd.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The initial value in integer register rs1 is treated as a bit mask that specifies bit positions to be cleared in the CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Any bit that is high in rs1 will cause the corresponding bit to be cleared in the CSR, if that CSR bit is writable.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: For both CSRRS and CSRRC, if rs1=`x0`, then the instruction will not write to the CSR at all, and so shall not cause any of the side effects that might otherwise occur on a CSR write, nor raise illegal-instruction exceptions on accesses to read-only CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Both CSRRS and CSRRC always read the addressed CSR and cause any read side effects regardless of rs1 and rd fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Note that if rs1 specifies a register other than `x0`, and that register holds a zero value, the instruction will not action any attendant per-field side effects, but will action any side effects caused by writing to the entire CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: A CSRRW with rs1=`x0` will attempt to write zero to the destination CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The CSRRWI, CSRRSI, and CSRRCI variants are similar to CSRRW, CSRRS, and CSRRC respectively, except they update the CSR using an XLEN-bit value obtained by zero-extending a 5-bit unsigned immediate (uimm[4:0]) field encoded in the rs1 field instead of a value from an integer register.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: For CSRRSI and CSRRCI, if the uimm[4:0] field is zero, then these instructions will not write to the CSR, and shall not cause any of the side effects that might otherwise occur on a CSR write, nor raise illegal-instruction exceptions on accesses to read-only CSRs.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: For CSRRWI, if rd=`x0`, then the instruction shall not read the CSR and shall not cause any of the side effects that might occur on a CSR read.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Both CSRRSI and CSRRCI will always read the CSR and cause any read side effects regardless of rd and rs1 fields.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: .Conditions determining whether a CSR instruction reads or writes the specified CSR. | 5+^|*Register operand* |Instruction |rd is `x0` |rs1 is `x0` |Reads CSR |Writes CSR
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: |CSRRS/CSRRC |- |Yes |Yes |No
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: |CSRRS/CSRRC |- |No |Yes |Yes
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: |Instruction |rd is `x0` |uimm=0 |Reads CSR |Writes CSR
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: |CSRRSI/CSRRCI |- |Yes |Yes |No
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: |CSRRSI/CSRRCI |- |No |Yes |Yes |
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: summarizes the behavior of the CSR instructions with respect to whether they read and/or write the CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: In addition to side effects that occur as a consequence of reading or writing a CSR, individual fields within a CSR might have side effects when written.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The CSRRW[I] instructions action side effects for all such fields within the written CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The CSRRS[I] and CSRRC[I] instructions only action side effects for fields for which the rs1 or uimm argument has at least one bit set corresponding to that field.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: For any event or consequence that occurs due to a CSR having a particular value, if a write to the CSR gives it that value, the resulting event or consequence is said to be an indirect effect of the write.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Indirect effects of a CSR write are not considered by the RISC-V ISA to be side effects of that write.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Some CSRs, such as the instructions-retired counter, `instret`, may be modified as side effects of instruction execution.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: In these cases, if a CSR access instruction reads a CSR, it reads the value prior to the execution of the instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: If a CSR access instruction writes such a CSR, the explicit write is done instead of the update from the side effect.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: In particular, a value written to `instret` by one instruction will be the value read by the following instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The assembler pseudoinstruction to read a CSR, CSRR rd, csr, is encoded as CSRRS rd, csr, x0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: The assembler pseudoinstruction to write a CSR, CSRW csr, rs1, is encoded as CSRRW x0, csr, rs1, while CSRWI csr, uimm, is encoded as CSRRWI x0, csr, uimm.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Further assembler pseudoinstructions are defined to set and clear bits in the CSR when the old value is not required: CSRS/CSRC csr, rs1; CSRSI/CSRCI csr, uimm.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions

sentences.text: Each RISC-V hart normally observes its own CSR accesses, including its implicit CSR accesses, as performed in program order.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: In particular, unless specified otherwise, a CSR access is performed after the execution of any prior instructions in program order whose behavior modifies or is modified by the CSR state and before the execution of any subsequent instructions in program order whose behavior modifies or is modified by the CSR state.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: Furthermore, an explicit CSR read returns the CSR state before the execution of the instruction, while an explicit CSR write suppresses and overrides any implicit writes or modifications to the same CSR by the same instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: Likewise, any side effects from an explicit CSR access are normally observed to occur synchronously in program order.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: Unless specified otherwise, the full consequences of any such side effects are observable by the very next instruction, and no consequences may be observed out-of-order by preceding instructions. (Note the distinction made earlier between side effects and indirect effects of CSR writes.)
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: For the RVWMO memory consistency model (), CSR accesses are weakly ordered by default, so other harts or devices may observe CSR accesses in an order different from program order.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: In addition, CSR accesses are not ordered with respect to explicit memory accesses, unless a CSR access modifies the execution behavior of the instruction that performs the explicit memory access or unless a CSR access and an explicit memory access are ordered by either the syntactic dependencies defined by the memory model or the ordering requirements defined in .
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: To enforce ordering in all other cases, software should execute a FENCE instruction between the relevant accesses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: For the purposes of the FENCE instruction, CSR read accesses are classified as device input (I), and CSR write accesses are classified as device output (O).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering

sentences.text: The hardware platform may define that accesses to certain CSRs are strongly ordered, Accesses to strongly ordered CSRs have stronger ordering constraints with respect to accesses to both weakly ordered CSRs and accesses to memory-mapped I/O regions.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Zicsr" Extension for Control and Status Register (CSR) Instructions, Version 2.0 > CSR Instructions > CSR Access Ordering
